---
title: "1.7 Application Program Interface and Libraries"
subtitle: "Using Objects and Methods"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit1-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Topic Intro

### APIs let you use existing code

We have already used `System.out.println()`. That method is part of the Java <span class="term">API</span> (Application Programming Interface).

::: {.tight-list}
- A <span class="term">library</span> is a collection of classes written by other programmers.
- An <span class="term">API specification</span> tells programmers how to use classes and methods in a library.
- A <span class="term">package</span> organizes related classes so they can be imported and reused.
:::

The practical point is simple: <span class="mark">you do not write everything from scratch</span>.

## Library, API, Package

### Similar terms, different emphasis

| Term | Classroom meaning |
|---|---|
| <span class="term">library</span> | collection of reusable classes or code |
| <span class="term">API</span> | specification that explains how to use the library |
| <span class="term">package</span> | Java's way to group related classes |
| <span class="term">class</span> | building block that defines a reference type |
| <span class="term">method</span> | block of code that performs a task |

These words often appear together in documentation.

## java.lang Package

### Built-in classes you already use

The `java.lang` package is available by default in Java.

::: {.tight-list}
- `String` is in `java.lang`.
- `System` is in `java.lang`.
- `System.out` is an object whose type is `PrintStream`.
- `PrintStream` provides `println()` methods for printing different value types.
:::

Documentation is essential because it tells you the available attributes and behaviors.

## Documentation Check

### How many `println` methods?

The `PrintStream` class includes overloaded `println` methods for different types.

Quick check:

::: {.tight-list}
- Find only methods named `println`.
- Do not count `print` or `printf`.
- Count each distinct `println` method signature.
:::

Answer: `10`.

Reason: there is one `println` method for each type it can print, plus the no-argument newline version.

## Quick Check

### Purpose of APIs and libraries

What is the purpose of APIs and libraries in programming?

::: {.tight-list}
- A. To write every new program from scratch
- B. To use code written by others
- C. To create programming languages
- D. To compile code
:::

Answer: <span class="mark">B</span>. APIs and libraries let programmers reuse tested code and focus on the specific task they are solving.

## Turtle Library

### A concrete API example

The CSAwesome2 `Turtle` library lets students create drawings using an animated turtle that moves around the screen.

![Turtle attributes and behaviors](../../assets/1-using-objects-and-methods/1-7-application-program-interface-and-libraries/turtleOOD.png){fig-align="center" width="54%"}

`Turtle` is a <span class="term">class</span> that can be used as a reference type. A turtle object such as `yertle` has data and behavior.

## Attributes and Behaviors

### Data versus actions

::: {.tight-list}
- <span class="term">Attributes</span> store data related to the class, such as `name`, `width`, `height`, or color.
- <span class="term">Behaviors</span> are what objects can do and are defined by methods.
- Methods always have parentheses, such as `forward()` or `turnLeft()`.
- Attributes do not have parentheses.
:::

This is a documentation-reading skill: parentheses usually signal a method.

## Dot Operator

### Ask an object to run a method

The <span class="term">dot operator</span> `.` connects an object or class with the method being used.

```java
yertle.forward();
yertle.turnRight();
System.out.println("Hi");
```

Read `yertle.forward()` as: ask the turtle object `yertle` to execute its `forward()` behavior.

## Code Task

### Turtle API starter code

Run the program, then add one more line to make `yertle` go `forward()` again.

```{.java .smaller}
import java.awt.*;
import java.util.*;

public class TurtleTest
{
    public static void main(String[] args)
    {
        World habitat = new World(300, 300);
        Turtle yertle = new Turtle(habitat);

        yertle.forward();
        yertle.turnRight();

        // Add another yertle.forward() call here.

        habitat.show(true);
    }
}
```

## Code Task

### Turtle API completion check

A successful solution should contain at least two calls to:

```java
yertle.forward();
```

The key reading is:

::: {.tight-list}
- `Turtle` is the class.
- `yertle` is an object of type `Turtle`.
- `forward()` and `turnRight()` are methods.
- `habitat.show(true)` displays the world.
:::

## Quick Checks

### Turtle vocabulary

| Question | Answer |
|---|---|
| Which way does a turtle face when first created? | North |
| What type of thing is `Turtle` in the program? | class |
| What type of thing is `turnRight`? | method |
| What type of thing is the turtle's position? | attribute |
| What are attributes of a class? | data stored in variables |

Use the class/object/attribute/method distinction every time you read API docs.

## Mixed-Up Code {.smaller}

### Draw a sideways capital L

![Sideways L target](../../assets/1-using-objects-and-methods/1-7-application-program-interface-and-libraries/turtleForwardLeftForward.png){fig-align="right" width="24%"}

Put the needed blocks in order.

```java
import java.util.*;
import java.awt.*;
public class TurtleTest {
    public static void main(String[] args) {
        World habitat = new World(300, 300);
        Turtle yertle = new Turtle(habitat);
        yertle.turnRight();
        yertle.forward();
        yertle.turnLeft();
        yertle.forward(50);
        habitat.show(true);
    }
}
```

Reject distractors such as `yertle.right();` and `yertle.forward()` without the semicolon.

## Groupwork Coding Challenge

### Turtle Drawing starter

Make `yertle` draw a shape by calling `forward`, `backward`, and turn methods several times.

```java
import java.awt.*;
import java.util.*;

public class TurtleShape
{
    public static void main(String[] args)
    {
        World habitat = new World(500, 500);
        Turtle yertle = new Turtle(habitat);

        // Use yertle's movement and turn methods to draw a shape.

        habitat.show(true);
    }
}
```

## Groupwork Coding Challenge

### Turtle Drawing checks

Your shape can be a square, zigzag, or block letter.

A strong solution should include:

::: {.tight-list}
- at least <span class="mark">3 turns</span> using `turnRight()` or `turnLeft()`
- at least <span class="mark">4 moves</span> using `forward()` or `backward()`
- enough code lines to show a deliberate drawing plan
- method calls written with the object name, dot operator, method name, parentheses, and semicolon
:::

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should...

::: {.tight-list}
- distinguish <span class="term">library</span>, <span class="term">API</span>, and <span class="term">package</span>
- identify `java.lang` as a built-in package
- explain that a class defines a reference type and can be used to create objects
- distinguish <span class="term">attributes</span> from <span class="term">methods</span>
- recognize methods by parentheses in documentation
- explain `object.method()` using the <span class="term">dot operator</span>
- use API documentation to decide which method call is valid
:::

## End

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)
